guardrail_evaluations.explanation: The proposed 30% discount exceeds the mid-market soft ceiling of 20%. This is a yellow flag — the AE should be prepared to justify the concession depth against the retention value of the Northbeam logo.
The discount is exactly at the 30% Finance approval threshold (<=30 passes). However, this is right at the guardrail ceiling — Finance sign-off is still required before this deal can close.
No ramp schedule is present on this deal, so ramp length is 0 months — well within the 3-month mid-market ceiling. No ramp-related approval needed.

Estimated gross margin of ~14.3% is far below the 40% warn floor. At this price, we are recovering only marginally above COGS — the deal needs to be restructured or the retention rationale made explicit.

Estimated gross margin of ~14.3% breaches the absolute 25% floor — this deal is structurally non-viable as proposed and cannot proceed without a fundamental reshape. The proposed price of $280K must be raised or concessions restructured before re-quoting.

alternative_structures.label: 24-Month Commitment at Reduced Discount
alternative_structures.description: Customer commits to a 24-month renewal at $320K ACV ($640K TCV) in exchange for a 20% discount off list. The longer term de-risks churn, improves LTV, and lands within the mid-market warn threshold. Payment terms remain net-60 as a goodwill concession. Usage-based overage stays in place above commit.
alternative_structures.rationale: Trading a longer commitment for a shallower discount brings gross margin exactly to the absolute floor at 25%, satisfies the Finance approval guardrail without breaching it, and doubles total contract value. The 24-month lock-in also neutralizes near-term competitor risk from the YC startup, which may not be proven at scale by mid-2027.

alternative_structures.label: Right-Sized Commit + Escalator Ramp
alternative_structures.description: Reset Year 1 commit to $252K (reflecting actual 60% utilization of the old $400K contract, i.e., ~$240K usage, rounded up 5% as a floor signal). Apply a contractual escalator to $300K in Year 2 if usage exceeds 80% of Year 1 commit. No upfront discount beyond the right-sizing; framed as a 'utilization-aligned renewal' rather than a price cut. Net-60 payment terms granted.
alternative_structures.rationale: This structure is the most customer-friendly in Year 1 but is included to illustrate the risk: right-sizing to actual usage collapses margin below even the absolute floor. It is presented here as a guardrail-failing option to anchor the conversation — the escalator in Year 2 is the structural offset the AE must negotiate hard for. Only viable if Year 2 escalator is contractually binding.

alternative_structures.label: Commit Reduction + Professional Services Unbundling
alternative_structures.description: Accept the $280K base commit reduction (30% discount) but unbundle a $40K Professional Services engagement (adoption acceleration: onboarding the 3 unadopted paid features) billed separately at full margin. Effective combined revenue = $320K. Net-60 payment on SaaS; net-30 on PS. CSM is already engaged, so PS scope is pre-qualified. Usage overage model remains.
alternative_structures.rationale: This structure accepts the customer's headline number on the SaaS line (reducing friction) while recovering $40K in full-margin PS revenue, bringing blended ACV to $320K. The PS scope directly addresses the under-utilization root cause — 3 unadopted features — which also improves health score, reducing future churn risk. SaaS margin remains below the absolute floor on its own, so Finance must be briefed that the blended deal economics are the unit of analysis.

pricing.reasoning_summary: The proposed $280K renewal represents a 30% discount off the $400K list price — exactly at the mid-market Finance approval ceiling but in breach of the soft warn (20%) and, critically, the absolute gross margin floor (25%). Under the working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin (COGS = $240K), the proposed price of $280K yields an estimated gross margin of only ~14.3% — $40K above COGS — which triggers a block_absolute and makes this deal structurally non-viable as submitted. The 40% list-price margin assumption is a CFO-level input that should be confirmed before final disposition. All three alternative structures are designed to bring the deal above the 25% margin floor, with the 24-month commitment path ($320K ACV) being the cleanest path to compliance; the PS unbundling path is the most politically viable given the CSM's existing customer relationship and the clear adoption gap.

performance_obligations.name: Platform subscription (committed base)
performance_obligations.description: Annual subscription to the Northbeam platform at a reduced $280K commit for the 12-month renewal term.
performance_obligations.rationale: The platform provides a stand-ready service over the contract term and is capable of being distinct and separately identifiable per ASC 606-10-25-19.

performance_obligations.name: Usage-based overage
performance_obligations.description: Per-unit overage billed at $0.0008 for consumption above the reduced commit.
performance_obligations.rationale: Overage charges represent variable consideration tied to customer usage and are recognized as the customer consumes the service (ASC 606-10-32-40 series allocation exception likely applies).

variable_consideration_flags.treatment_required: recognize_as_consumed (series guidance / variable consideration allocated to usage)
variable_consideration_flags.explanation: Customer used only 60% of prior commit, so probability of overage is low. Recognize overage revenue as usage occurs rather than estimating upfront.
The 30% commit reduction itself is fixed for FY26, but sets a precedent that may influence future renewals; for FY26 the transaction price is fixed at $280K.
Net-60 terms are unlikely to constitute a significant financing component under ASC 606-10-32-15 through 32-18, but should be confirmed.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: The renewal is a new annual contract with fixed commit and a defined overage rate. No MFN, TFC, or expansion-pricing-lock clauses are present that would re-open the contract mid-term. The shift to usage for overage is contemplated in the contract itself, not a future modification trigger.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Committed subscription recognized ratably over the 12-month term.
Overage above commit recognized as consumed at $0.0008/unit; expected to be ~$0 given 60% historical utilization.

red_flags.label: Steep 30% discount with no commit-floor justification
red_flags.explanation: A 30% price concession on a renewal where the customer consumed only 60% of prior commit warrants CFO review; ensure SSP and discount documentation support revenue allocation.

red_flags.label: Net-60 payment terms — financing component assessment
red_flags.explanation: Extension from net-30 to net-60 should be assessed for a significant financing component and for credit/collectibility (ASC 606-10-25-1(e)), particularly given customer health score 42 and recent layoffs/secondary.

red_flags.label: Collectibility risk from customer financial signals
red_flags.explanation: Layoffs, down-round secondary, and sector softness suggest collectibility should be reassessed before contract inception under ASC 606-10-25-1(e).

red_flags.label: Hybrid model transition
red_flags.explanation: Shift to usage-based overage requires clear metering, billing, and SSP documentation to support revenue recognition timing.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This is a 12-month renewal with a fixed $280K commit recognized ratably and a usage-based overage component recognized as consumed. Key accounting considerations are collectibility given the customer's deteriorating financial signals, the net-60 financing-component assessment, and documentation supporting the 30% discount for SSP allocation. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests a 30% reduction in their annual committed spend, from $400K to $280K, citing that they consumed only 60% of their prior-year commit.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A 30% commit reduction sets a dangerous renewal precedent: Clay absorbs a $120K ARR haircut on a customer whose under-utilization is a product adoption failure, not a pricing failure. If Clay concedes the full reduction without addressing root-cause adoption, the same argument recurs at next renewal — with the new $280K baseline as the anchor. At 60% consumption, the customer still derived value from 60% of a $400K product; the correct lever is adoption remediation, not ACV reduction.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay agrees to a one-year renewal at $320,000 ACV (20% reduction from $400,000 list), conditioned upon Customer's written commitment to a 90-day joint success plan ('JSP') executed within 30 days of contract signature. The JSP shall identify no fewer than three (3) currently unadopted paid features, assign Customer-side DRIs, and establish monthly utilization checkpoints. Should Customer achieve ≥80% commit consumption by month nine (9) of the renewal term, the parties agree to negotiate the FY27 renewal at no less than $340,000 ACV.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept $280K ACV only if Customer co-signs the JSP with executive sponsor named, and agrees to a contractual floor of $320K ACV for the FY27 renewal ('step-up clause'), with a mutual termination right at month six if utilization remains below 50%.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has accepted commit reductions of up to 20% on at-risk renewals with documented health-score improvement plans; 30% reductions without conditions have not been approved at the deal-desk level.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests that any consumption above the new $280K committed baseline be billed at a usage-based overage rate ($0.0008/unit) rather than locked into a higher commit tier.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Shifting overage to pure pay-as-you-go at $0.0008/unit removes Clay's ability to upsell into a higher commit tier mid-term, and at a customer health score of 42 the probability of meaningful overage is low — meaning this clause carries almost no upside for Clay while eliminating a key expansion motion. If utilization recovers and the customer runs hot, they benefit from a metered rate with no commit obligation. This also creates billing complexity and cash-flow unpredictability for a net-60 payer.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Overage above the $[280,000] annual commit shall be billed at $0.0010 per unit (standard overage rate), invoiced monthly in arrears. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Customer's trailing 60-day utilization rate exceeds 90% of the annual commit on an annualized basis, Clay reserves the right to present Customer with a mid-term commit uplift offer; Customer retains the right to accept or decline such offer. Pure usage-based billing with no floor is not available under this agreement.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept $0.0008/unit overage rate but require that any overage invoice is due net-30 (not net-60) and that total overage charges are capped at 30% of ACV per year before a mandatory commit renegotiation is triggered.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered hybrid commit-plus-overage structures to fintech accounts; pure usage-based overage without a secondary commit step-up has been declined in at least two prior deal-desk reviews.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests extension of payment terms from net-30 to net-60 across all invoices for the renewal term.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Net-60 on a $280K ACV deal means Clay floats approximately $23K/month for an additional 30 days relative to standard terms. More critically, combined with the customer's 8% workforce reduction, below-mark Series C secondary, and CRO emphasis on 'cost discipline,' the counterparty risk profile is elevated — net-60 increases Clay's exposure window in the event of a payment default or further financial deterioration at Northbeam.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Payment terms shall be net-45 from invoice date. Customer shall provide Clay with a valid ACH or credit card authorization on file within 10 business days of contract execution to facilitate timely payment. Invoices unpaid beyond net-45 shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month. Clay's standard net-30 terms apply to any renewal or expansion order executed after the initial term.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept net-60 only if Customer agrees to annual upfront invoicing (single invoice due net-60 at term start) rather than quarterly billing, which reduces Clay's collection overhead and locks in the full-year cash receipt in one payment.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Net-45 has been accepted as a compromise position on two prior at-risk renewals; net-60 with quarterly billing has not been approved without a credit review.

redline.one_line_summary: We can retain Northbeam, but we should counter at $320K (not $280K) with a mandatory success plan, hold overage at $0.0010/unit, and push payment terms to net-45 or annual upfront — accepting all three CSM-proposed concessions as-is sets a damaging precedent and leaves $120K ARR on the table from a customer whose problem is adoption, not pricing.
redline.reasoning_summary: The headline risk here is not any single non-standard clause but the combination of all three concessions being accepted simultaneously with no adoption condition attached. Northbeam's under-utilization is a product adoption failure — 3 of 5 features unadopted — not evidence that the product is overpriced. Customer signals (8% layoffs, below-mark secondary, CRO 'cost discipline' language, 14% YoY origination decline) confirm genuine budget pressure but also confirm Clay is being used as a relief valve. The YC competitor at 60% below is a real threat but is unproven at scale; Clay's strategic counter should trade a modest ACV concession for a binding success plan that addresses the root cause and protects the FY27 anchor price. Payment term extension to net-60 compounds counterparty risk given Northbeam's financial trajectory and should be negotiated to net-45 or annual upfront.

required_approvers.rationale: Default approver; all deals require AE Manager sign-off as the first step in the approval chain.
Proposed 30% discount exceeds the 25% threshold and triggers CFO review; Pricing Agent also confirms discount is at the mid-market Finance approval ceiling with block_absolute margin violation.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Non-standard payment terms route to Finance
required_approvers.rationale: Non-standard clause 'payment_terms_net_60' shifts cash timing by 30 days and requires Finance assessment of collectibility and ASC 606 financing-component treatment, particularly given customer's health score of 42.
Deal ACV of $280K falls within the $100K–$500K band and requires RevOps review for structure consistency and contract admin.

Redline Agent marked overall_redline_priority as 'high'; flagged clauses (commit_reduction, usage_based_overage, payment_terms) require Legal review to negotiate counter-positions and document precedent risk.

blockers_to_address_first: Pricing Agent confirms deal is in breach of absolute gross margin floor (14.3% < 25% required). AE must either restructure the deal (e.g., 24-month commitment at $320K ACV, or PS unbundling to $320K blended ACV) or obtain explicit CFO override with documented retention rationale before submitting.

Redline Agent flagged 30% commit reduction as a dangerous precedent without adoption conditions. AE must negotiate counter-position: either reduce discount to 20% ($320K ACV) with mandatory 90-day joint success plan addressing the three unadopted features, or accept $280K only if customer co-signs JSP with step-up clause for FY27 at no less than $320K ACV.
Redline Agent requires overage rate negotiation from customer's requested $0.0008/unit to standard $0.0010/unit, or fallback to $0.0008/unit with net-30 invoicing on overage and 30% annual cap.

Redline Agent requires payment terms negotiation from net-60 to net-45 (or annual upfront invoicing). Net-60 on a $280K deal floats $23K/month for 30 additional days; combined with customer's 8% layoffs, below-mark secondary, and CRO cost-discipline language, counterparty risk is elevated.

ASC 606 Agent flagged collectibility risk from customer's deteriorating financial signals (8% workforce reduction, secondary at below-mark valuation, 14% YoY origination decline, health score 42). CFO must complete a credit/financial-stability review before contract inception per ASC 606-10-25-1(e).

Finance must confirm whether the list-price gross margin assumption of 40% (COGS = $240K) is accurate. If margin is lower, the structural breach is even more acute and the deal may be non-viable without restructuring.
approval.one_line_summary: This deal routes to AE Manager → CFO + Finance + RevOps + Legal (parallel, all required). Expected cycle time ~5 business days. Blocker: deal breaches absolute margin floor (14.3% < 25%), Redline Agent requires counter-positions on all three customer concessions (commit, overage, terms), and CFO must complete collectibility review. AE must restructure to $320K ACV (24-month term or PS unbundling) with mandatory adoption success plan, negotiate overage to $0.0010/unit and payment terms to net-45, before this deal can be submitted for approval.
approval.reasoning_summary: The deal meets multiple approval thresholds (30% discount, $280K ACV, net-60 payment terms, high-priority redline flags) and routes to five approvers running in parallel after AE Manager. However, confidence is low due to three critical structural risks: (1) Pricing Agent explicitly states the deal breaches the absolute gross margin floor at 14.3% and is 'structurally non-viable as proposed,' requiring either price restructuring or margin override; (2) Redline Agent flags the 30% commit reduction as a dangerous precedent that absorbs a $120K ARR haircut without adoption remediation and does not recommend accepting all three CSM concessions; (3) ASC 606 Agent and Redline Agent both raise collectibility risk given Northbeam's financial deterioration (layoffs, secondary, cost discipline, health score 42), which CFO must formally clear. The AE must resolve these blockers—by restructuring to $320K ACV with binding success plan, counter-negotiating redline positions, and obtaining CFO credit sign-off—before submitting for approval.

text.text: Deal Review: FY26 Renewal — Commit Reduction Request · Northbeam Mortgage

fields.text:
*Estimated Gross Margin*
~14.3% — breaches absolute floor of 25%

*Approval Chain*
AE Manager → CFO + Finance + RevOps + Legal (parallel) · ~5 biz days

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know*

• *Deal is structurally non-viable as submitted.* Estimated gross margin of 14.3% breaches the absolute 25% floor — this is a block, not a warn. The deal cannot proceed to approval without restructuring. Recommended path: counter at $320K ACV (20% discount) on a 24-month term, or accept $280K base only with a $40K PS unbundling engagement, bringing blended ACV to $320K. The CSM's proposal to accept all three concessions as-is must not go forward.

• *Under-utilization is an adoption problem, not a pricing problem.* Northbeam consumed 60% of their $400K commit, but 3 of 5 paid features are unadopted. Granting a 30% reduction without a binding joint success plan sets a $280K anchor for FY27 and absorbs a $120K ARR haircut for a root cause Clay can fix. Any discount concession must be conditioned on a co-signed 90-day JSP with a named executive sponsor and a contractual FY27 step-up floor.

• *Counterparty risk is elevated — CFO collectibility review required before inception.* Customer signals are materially negative: 8% workforce reduction (Mar 2026), a below-mark Series C secondary ($40M structured, Nov 2025), CRO publicly emphasizing 'cost discipline' eight times on the Q4 call, and mortgage origination volume down 14% YoY. Health score is 42/100. Net-60 payment terms compound exposure. ASC 606 collectibility must be formally cleared by CFO under ASC 606-10-25-1(e) before contract execution.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Jamal Okafor · *AE Manager:* Marcus Reilly · *Segment:* Mid-Market · *Close Date:* 2026-05-18

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
DEAL REVIEW — FY26 Renewal: Northbeam Mortgage | AE: Jamal Okafor
Proposed ACV: $280K (30% disc. off $400K list) · Gross margin: ~14.3% — BREACHES absolute 25% floor. Deal is non-viable as submitted.
Approval chain: AE Manager → CFO + Finance + RevOps + Legal (parallel, ~5 biz days). CFO has veto; Legal has veto.
Key actions required: (1) Restructure to $320K ACV (24-mo or PS unbundling); (2) Condition any discount on co-signed joint success plan targeting 3 unadopted features; (3) CFO must complete ASC 606 collectibility review before contract inception given health score 42, 8% layoffs, and below-mark secondary.
Do not submit for approval until all three blockers are resolved. Review filed by Kiln · agent-driven.

ae_email_draft.subject: [Northbeam] Deal-desk review complete — restructure required before approval submission
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Jamal, the Northbeam renewal **cannot be submitted as proposed** — the $280K/30% discount structure breaches the absolute gross margin floor (14.3% vs. 25% required) and is a hard block. Here's what you need to do before this goes anywhere:

1. **Restructure the deal to $320K ACV.** Either (a) counter with a 24-month commitment at $320K ACV (20% discount, clean margin floor), or (b) accept the $280K SaaS line but unbundle a $40K PS engagement covering the 3 unadopted features — blended ACV = $320K. The PS path may be easiest given the CSM is already engaged and the adoption gap is documented.
2. **Counter on all three non-standard clauses.** Commit reduction: $320K with a mandatory 90-day Joint Success Plan and a $320K FY27 step-up floor (or accept $280K only with a signed JSP + step-up). Overage rate: push from $0.0008 to $0.0010/unit (or cap overage invoicing at net-30 as fallback). Payment terms: counter at net-45, or accept net-60 only if customer agrees to annual upfront invoicing.
3. **Brief CFO on collectibility before submitting.** Northbeam has an 8% headcount cut, a below-mark Series C secondary, a health score of 42, and a CRO who said "cost discipline" eight times on the Q4 call — Finance needs a formal credit review cleared per ASC 606-10-25-1(e) before contract inception.

Approval chain once restructured: **Marcus (AE Manager) → then CFO + Finance + RevOps + Legal in parallel.** CFO has veto rights; Legal has veto rights. Budget ~5 business days from clean submission. Close date is May 18 — you need a restructured term sheet back to the customer no later than end of day Thursday to have any shot at the approval cycle completing in time.

customer_email_draft.subject: Northbeam FY26 Renewal — Our Revised Proposal
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
We understand Northbeam is navigating a genuinely difficult macro environment — sustained rate pressure, tighter budget scrutiny, and real cost discipline across the business — and we want to find a path that reflects that reality while keeping the partnership intact.

On the commit level: we cannot accept a reduction to $280K as structured because it doesn't address the root cause, which is adoption, not price — three of five paid features remain unused, and a straight ACV cut doesn't change that trajectory. Our counter is a renewal at **$320,000 ACV** (20% off list), paired with a 90-day joint success plan executed within 30 days of signature, with named DRIs on your side and monthly utilization checkpoints; if you hit ≥80% consumption by month nine, we'll anchor the FY27 renewal floor at $340K.

On overage: we're aligned on moving to a usage-based model above commit, and we'll hold the rate at **$0.0010/unit** (our standard overage rate) rather than the $0.0008 requested — the difference is modest and keeps the billing structure consistent with how we handle this across the book.

On payment terms: we can move from net-30, but our counter is **net-45** rather than net-60; given the invoicing cadence on a $320K deal, this keeps cash timing manageable for both sides, and we'll ask for an ACH authorization on file at signing.

The adoption plan isn't a formality — your CSM is already engaged, the feature gaps are identified, and closing them is the single biggest lever on your health score and on making the FY27 conversation easier than this one.

To hit your CRO's signing window, we need alignment on these terms by **May 12** so our internal approvals can clear in time. Can we get 30 minutes this week to walk through the success plan scope and confirm the commercial terms?

approval_review_one_pager.title: FY26 Renewal — Northbeam Mortgage Commit Reduction Request

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Northbeam Mortgage (850 employees, Series C, fintech/mortgage) is requesting a 30% ACV reduction ($400K → $280K), a shift to usage-based overage, and net-60 payment terms at renewal. CSM recommends accepting all three; deal desk recommends a structured counter at $320K ACV with binding adoption conditions.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| List Price | $400,000 |
| Proposed Price (CSM ask) | $280,000 |
| Effective Discount | 30% |
| Estimated Gross Margin at $280K | ~14.3% |
| Margin Floor (absolute) | 25% |
| Margin Floor (warn) | 40% |

**The $280K proposal breaches the absolute gross margin floor (14.3% vs. 25% required) and is structurally non-viable as submitted.**

**Alternative structures to close the margin gap:**
- **Option A (Recommended):** 24-month commitment at $320K ACV (20% discount) — margin at exactly 25% floor, doubles TCV, neutralizes YC competitor near-term.
- **Option B:** Accept $280K SaaS base + unbundle $40K Professional Services (3 unadopted features) → $320K blended ACV; SaaS margin still sub-floor, Finance must approve on blended basis.
- **Option C (Guardrail-failing, do not ship):** Right-size to $252K actual usage → margin collapses to 4.8%; included only as anchor illustration.

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**Redline Flags (Priority: HIGH)**
- **Commit reduction (High):** 30% haircut with no adoption condition absorbs $120K ARR on a customer whose problem is feature adoption failure (3 of 5 paid features unused), not pricing. Sets a $280K anchor for FY27. Counter: $320K ACV + mandatory 90-day Joint Success Plan (JSP) with executive DRI; fallback $280K only with contractual FY27 step-up floor of $320K.
- **Usage-based overage (Medium):** Pure pay-as-you-go at $0.0008/unit eliminates mid-term upsell motion; given 42/100 health score, overage probability is near zero — all downside, no upside for Clay. Counter: $0.0010/unit standard rate; fallback $0.0008 with net-30 invoicing on overage and 30% annual cap.
- **Net-60 payment terms (Medium):** Floats ~$23K/month for 30 additional days. Combined with 8% layoffs, below-mark secondary, and CRO "cost discipline" language, counterparty risk is elevated. Counter: net-45 + ACH on file; fallback annual upfront invoice due net-60.

**ASC 606 Flags**
- **Warn:** 30% discount requires SSP documentation and CFO sign-off for revenue allocation defensibility.
- **Warn:** Net-60 terms require assessment for significant financing component (ASC 606-10-32-15).
- **Info:** Collectibility risk — layoffs, down-round secondary, origination volume -14% YoY, health score 42 — must be formally cleared at contract inception per ASC 606-10-25-1(e).
- **Info:** Hybrid model transition requires metering, billing, and SSP documentation for usage overage recognition.

**Customer Signals (all negative)**
- 8% workforce reduction (Mar 2026) — back-office and loan officers
- Series C secondary at below-2023 mark ($40M, runway extension framing)
- CRO cited "cost discipline" 8x on Q4 investor call
- Mortgage origination volume -14% YoY for third straight quarter
- YC W26 competitor pitching at ~60% below Clay pricing (unproven at scale)

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Veto? | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | AE Manager (Marcus Reilly) | No | Default first step |
| 2 (parallel) | CFO | **Yes** | Discount >25%; absolute margin breach |
| 2 (parallel) | Finance | No | Non-standard payment terms (net-60); ASC 606 collectibility review |
| 2 (parallel) | RevOps | No | ACV $100K–$500K band |
| 2 (parallel) | Legal | **Yes** | Redline priority flagged HIGH; all three clauses require counter-negotiation |

**Estimated cycle time: ~5 business days** (assumes AE resolves blockers before submission)

**Pre-submission blockers AE must clear first:**
1. Restructure deal to ≥$320K ACV (24-month term or PS unbundling) OR obtain explicit CFO margin override with documented retention rationale
2. Counter commit reduction with JSP condition or FY27 step-up clause
3. Counter overage rate to $0.0010/unit
4. Counter payment terms to net-45 or annual upfront
5. CFO must complete credit/collectibility review before submission

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**HOLD — Ship with Conditions**

Do not approve the CSM-proposed deal as submitted. The $280K proposal breaches the absolute gross margin floor, accepts all three customer concessions with no adoption guardrails, and sets a damaging renewal precedent on a customer whose core problem is feature adoption, not price. **Counter at $320K ACV (Option A: 24-month term at 20% discount) with a mandatory Joint Success Plan addressing the 3 unadopted features, overage held at $0.0010/unit, and payment terms negotiated to net-45 — then resubmit for approval.**

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to warm tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 3 flagged redlines (3 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~5 business days through 5-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing blocks the deal as submitted: the 30% discount drives estimated gross margin to ~14.3%, well below the 25% absolute floor, making the $280K structure structurally non-viable without a CFO margin override or restructure to ~$320K ACV. ASC 606 treatment is standard ratable-plus-usage, but collectibility is a live concern given Northbeam's layoffs, below-mark secondary, and health score of 42, and the net-60 term introduces a financing-component question that must be cleared. On redline, the core risk is accepting all three concessions simultaneously with no adoption remediation — under-utilization is a product adoption failure, not a pricing failure, and net-60 should be pulled back to net-45 or annual upfront. Approval routes through a five-step chain (AE Mgr → CFO → Finance → RevOps → Legal) over ~5 business days, but three blockers — margin floor breach, collectibility, and unconditioned concessions — must clear before submission. Recommendation: hold and re-scope to a $320K / 24-month structure with a binding success plan and tightened payment terms before re-entering approval.

similar_deals.decision_note: Cantilever's General Counsel is requiring (a) full audit log export weekly to their SOC/SIEM in CEF format, (b) a whitelist where any third-party data processor we route enrichment through has to be pre-approved (they sent the initial wh…
Coastline's regulatory audit window pushed procurement out 9 months. Champion left mid-cycle. Logged for re-engagement Q4 2026.

similar_deals.deal_name: Q2 Expansion — Inbound SDR AI Enrichment
similar_deals.decision_note: Ramp's RevOps wants to add Clay enrichment + AI scoring across their inbound SDR pipeline (45 reps). They're asking for an MFN limited to three named competitors (Apollo, Clearbit, ZoomInfo Sales OS), a 99.95% uptime SLA with service cre…

signals.headline: Northbeam Mortgage trims 8% of staff in operations and loan-officer roles
signals.summary: Northbeam Mortgage confirmed an 8% workforce reduction primarily affecting back-office operations and loan-officer roles, citing the prolonged rate environment and a need to extend runway through 2027.

signals.headline: YC-backed mortgage-origination startup launches at 60% below incumbents
signals.summary: A Y Combinator W26 fintech launched a mortgage-origination platform priced at roughly 60% below incumbents like Northbeam and Better, targeting mid-market regional lenders with an AI-first underwriting workflow.

signals.headline: Mortgage origination volume falls for third straight quarter as rates hold high
signals.summary: Industry data shows mortgage origination volume down 14% YoY in Q1 2026 as the Fed holds rates above 5%. Mid-market lenders cite a third straight quarter of demand softness.

signals.headline: Northbeam Mortgage CRO emphasizes 'cost discipline' on Q4 investor call
signals.summary: On the Q4 2025 investor call, Northbeam's CRO highlighted 'unit economics' and 'cost discipline' eight times, signaling tighter scrutiny on every renewal and procurement contract through fiscal 2026.

signals.headline: Northbeam Mortgage extends Series C runway with $40M secondary
signals.summary: Northbeam closed a $40M structured secondary led by an existing investor, framed as runway extension rather than a primary raise. The transaction valued the company below its 2023 Series C mark.
